tasks.title: Fix PPTX Generation Quality
tasks.description: Improve PowerPoint presentation generation to meet TAM Capital brand standards with proper slide layouts, charts, and consistent branding
tasks.details:
Update the PPTX generation module to:
1. Implement TAM Capital brand colors (#primary, #secondary from Tailwind config)
2. Add TAM letterhead/logo to title slide and headers
3. Improve chart generation using python-pptx chart capabilities
4. Create consistent slide layouts: Title, Section Header, Content, Chart, Table
5. Implement proper text formatting with Arabic RTL support
6. Add page numbers and date stamps

Pseudo-code:
```python
from pptx import Presentation
from pptx.util import Inches, Pt
from pptx.enum.text import PP_ALIGN
from pptx.chart.data import ChartData

class TAMPresentationGenerator:
    def __init__(self):
        self.prs = Presentation('tam_template.pptx')  # Use TAM branded template
        self.tam_colors = {
            'primary': RGBColor(0, 51, 102),  # TAM blue
            'secondary': RGBColor(255, 204, 0)  # TAM gold
        }
    
    def add_title_slide(self, title, subtitle, date):
        slide = self.prs.slides.add_slide(self.prs.slide_layouts[0])
        # Add TAM logo
        slide.shapes.add_picture('tam_logo.png', Inches(0.5), Inches(0.5))
        # Set title with brand font
        title_shape = slide.shapes.title
        title_shape.text = title
        title_shape.text_frame.paragraphs[0].font.color.rgb = self.tam_colors['primary']
        
    def add_financial_chart(self, data, chart_type='column'):
        slide = self.prs.slides.add_slide(self.prs.slide_layouts[5])
        chart_data = ChartData()
        # Populate chart with financial metrics
        # Apply TAM color scheme to chart
```

tasks.title: Fix PDF Generation Quality
tasks.description: Enhance PDF report generation with improved formatting, typography, page layout, and TAM Capital letterhead integration
tasks.details:
Refactor PDF generation to use ReportLab or WeasyPrint for better control:
1. Implement TAM letterhead on first page and headers
2. Set up proper page margins and typography (Arial/Helvetica for body, TAM fonts for headers)
3. Add page numbers, table of contents, and section bookmarks
4. Implement proper table formatting for financial data
5. Support bilingual content with Arabic RTL
6. Add charts/graphs as vector graphics

Pseudo-code:
```python
from reportlab.lib.pagesizes import A4
from reportlab.platypus import SimpleDocTemplate, Paragraph, Table, Image
from reportlab.lib.styles import getSampleStyleSheet, ParagraphStyle
from reportlab.lib.units import inch
from reportlab.pdfbase import pdfmetrics
from reportlab.pdfbase.ttfonts import TTFont

class TAMPDFGenerator:
    def __init__(self):
        # Register Arabic fonts
        pdfmetrics.registerFont(TTFont('Arabic', 'NotoSansArabic.ttf'))
        self.doc = SimpleDocTemplate('report.pdf', pagesize=A4)
        self.styles = self._create_tam_styles()
        
    def _create_tam_styles(self):
        styles = getSampleStyleSheet()
        # TAM header style
        styles.add(ParagraphStyle(
            name='TAMHeader',
            fontName='Helvetica-Bold',
            fontSize=16,
            textColor=colors.HexColor('#003366'),  # TAM blue
            spaceAfter=12
        ))
        return styles
        
    def add_letterhead(self, canvas, doc):
        # Draw TAM logo and letterhead
        canvas.drawImage('tam_letterhead.png', 0, 750, width=8.5*inch)
        canvas.setFont('Helvetica', 9)
        canvas.drawString(inch, 0.5*inch, f'Page {doc.page}')
```

tasks.title: Complete Report Comparison Feature
tasks.description: Finish implementing the side-by-side report comparison with comprehensive metrics tracking and change highlighting
tasks.details:
Complete the report comparison functionality:
1. Implement diff algorithm for financial metrics (percentage and absolute changes)
2. Create change tracking for text sections (executive summary, risks, outlook)
3. Build visual indicators for improvements/deteriorations (green/red arrows)
4. Add comparison summary dashboard
5. Implement date range selector for historical comparisons

Pseudo-code:
```python
from typing import Dict, List, Tuple
import difflib
from datetime import datetime

class ReportComparator:
    def __init__(self, report1_id: int, report2_id: int):
        self.report1 = self._load_report(report1_id)
        self.report2 = self._load_report(report2_id)
        
    def compare_metrics(self) -> Dict[str, Dict]:
        metrics_diff = {}
        for metric in ['revenue', 'earnings', 'pe_ratio', 'market_cap']:
            old_val = self.report1.get(metric, 0)
            new_val = self.report2.get(metric, 0)
            metrics_diff[metric] = {
                'old': old_val,
                'new': new_val,
                'change': new_val - old_val,
                'change_pct': ((new_val - old_val) / old_val * 100) if old_val else 0,
                'direction': 'up' if new_val > old_val else 'down'
            }
        return metrics_diff
        
    def compare_text_sections(self) -> Dict[str, List[Tuple]]:
        sections = ['executive_summary', 'risks', 'outlook']
        text_diffs = {}
        for section in sections:
            old_text = self.report1.get(section, '').split('\n')
            new_text = self.report2.get(section, '').split('\n')
            diff = list(difflib.unified_diff(old_text, new_text))
            text_diffs[section] = self._format_diff(diff)
        return text_diffs
```

tasks.title: Build Report Versioning System
tasks.description: Implement comprehensive version tracking for all generated reports with snapshot functionality per ticker
tasks.details:
Create versioning infrastructure:
1. Extend database schema to support report versions
2. Implement version numbering scheme (semantic or sequential)
3. Create snapshot mechanism to preserve full report state
4. Build version metadata tracking (created_by, created_at, change_summary)
5. Implement version branching for draft vs published

Database schema:
```sql
-- Add to existing schema
ALTER TABLE reports ADD COLUMN version_number INTEGER DEFAULT 1;
ALTER TABLE reports ADD COLUMN parent_version_id INTEGER REFERENCES reports(id);
ALTER TABLE reports ADD COLUMN version_status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'draft';
ALTER TABLE reports ADD COLUMN change_summary TEXT;

CREATE TABLE report_versions (
    id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    report_id INTEGER REFERENCES reports(id),
    version_number INTEGER NOT NULL,
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),
    created_by INTEGER REFERENCES users(id),
    snapshot_data JSONB NOT NULL,
    change_summary TEXT,
    UNIQUE(report_id, version_number)
);
```

Python implementation:
```python
class ReportVersionManager:
    def create_version(self, report_id: int, user_id: int) -> int:
        # Get current report
        current = db.query(Report).filter_by(id=report_id).first()
        
        # Create snapshot
        snapshot = {
            'metrics': current.metrics,
            'analysis': current.analysis,
            'generated_at': current.generated_at.isoformat(),
            'data_sources': [s.to_dict() for s in current.sources]
        }
        
        # Increment version
        new_version = current.version_number + 1
        
        # Create version record
        version = ReportVersion(
            report_id=report_id,
            version_number=new_version,
            created_by=user_id,
            snapshot_data=snapshot,
            change_summary=self._generate_change_summary(current)
        )
        db.session.add(version)
        db.session.commit()
        return version.id
```

tasks.title: Implement Version Diff Engine
tasks.description: Build a sophisticated difference engine to compare report versions and highlight changes in valuation, outlook, risks, and financials
tasks.details:
Create comprehensive diff engine:
1. Implement metric comparison with significance thresholds
2. Build text diff algorithm for narrative sections
3. Create change categorization (minor/major/critical)
4. Generate human-readable change summaries
5. Support bilingual diff output

Pseudo-code:
```python
from enum import Enum
import json
from typing import List, Dict, Any

class ChangeType(Enum):
    MINOR = 'minor'  # < 5% change
    MODERATE = 'moderate'  # 5-15% change
    MAJOR = 'major'  # > 15% change
    CRITICAL = 'critical'  # Rating change, outlook change

class VersionDiffEngine:
    def __init__(self, version1_id: int, version2_id: int):
        self.v1 = self._load_version(version1_id)
        self.v2 = self._load_version(version2_id)
        self.changes = []
        
    def analyze_changes(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        return {
            'metric_changes': self._compare_metrics(),
            'text_changes': self._compare_text(),
            'rating_changes': self._compare_ratings(),
            'summary': self._generate_summary(),
            'change_score': self._calculate_change_score()
        }
        
    def _compare_metrics(self) -> List[Dict]:
        changes = []
        metrics = ['revenue', 'ebitda', 'net_income', 'pe_ratio', 'pb_ratio']
        
        for metric in metrics:
            old = self.v1['metrics'].get(metric)
            new = self.v2['metrics'].get(metric)
            
            if old and new:
                pct_change = ((new - old) / old) * 100
                change_type = self._categorize_change(pct_change)
                
                changes.append({
                    'metric': metric,
                    'old_value': old,
                    'new_value': new,
                    'change_pct': pct_change,
                    'change_type': change_type.value,
                    'formatted': self._format_change(metric, old, new, pct_change)
                })
        return changes
        
    def _generate_summary(self) -> Dict[str, str]:
        # Generate bilingual summary
        en_summary = self._build_english_summary()
        ar_summary = self._build_arabic_summary()
        return {'en': en_summary, 'ar': ar_summary}
```

tasks.title: Build Analyst Watchlist Model
tasks.description: Create database models and API endpoints for analysts to maintain personal watchlists of tickers with monitoring preferences
tasks.details:
Implement watchlist functionality:
1. Create watchlist database schema
2. Build CRUD API endpoints
3. Implement watchlist limits and validation
4. Add monitoring preferences per ticker
5. Create watchlist sharing capabilities

Database schema:
```sql
CREATE TABLE watchlists (
    id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    user_id INTEGER REFERENCES users(id),
    name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    description TEXT,
    is_default BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),
    updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE TABLE watchlist_items (
    id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    watchlist_id INTEGER REFERENCES watchlists(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    ticker VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL,
    added_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),
    notes TEXT,
    alert_settings JSONB DEFAULT '{}',
    UNIQUE(watchlist_id, ticker)
);

CREATE TABLE watchlist_alerts (
    id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    watchlist_item_id INTEGER REFERENCES watchlist_items(id),
    alert_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
    threshold_value DECIMAL,
    comparison_operator VARCHAR(10),
    is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
    last_triggered TIMESTAMP,
    notification_channels JSONB DEFAULT '["in_app"]'
);
```

FastAPI implementation:
```python
from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, HTTPException
from sqlalchemy.orm import Session
from typing import List

router = APIRouter(prefix='/api/watchlists')

@router.post('/')
async def create_watchlist(
    name: str,
    description: str = None,
    user: User = Depends(get_current_user),
    db: Session = Depends(get_db)
):
    # Check watchlist limit (e.g., 10 per user)
    count = db.query(Watchlist).filter_by(user_id=user.id).count()
    if count >= 10:
        raise HTTPException(400, 'Watchlist limit reached')
        
    watchlist = Watchlist(
        user_id=user.id,
        name=name,
        description=description,
        is_default=(count == 0)
    )
    db.add(watchlist)
    db.commit()
    return watchlist

@router.post('/{watchlist_id}/tickers')
async def add_ticker(
    watchlist_id: int,
    ticker: str,
    alert_settings: dict = None,
    user: User = Depends(get_current_user),
    db: Session = Depends(get_db)
):
    # Validate ticker exists
    if not validate_ticker(ticker):
        raise HTTPException(400, 'Invalid ticker symbol')
        
    # Check ownership
    watchlist = db.query(Watchlist).filter_by(
        id=watchlist_id, 
        user_id=user.id
    ).first()
    if not watchlist:
        raise HTTPException(404, 'Watchlist not found')
        
    item = WatchlistItem(
        watchlist_id=watchlist_id,
        ticker=ticker.upper(),
        alert_settings=alert_settings or {}
    )
    db.add(item)
    db.commit()
    return item
```

tasks.title: Build Alert Engine and Notification System
tasks.description: Create comprehensive alert triggering and notification delivery system with in-app alerts and email digests
tasks.details:
Implement alert infrastructure:
1. Build alert triggering logic with cooldown periods
2. Create notification queue and delivery system
3. Implement in-app notification center
4. Build email digest templates (HTML + plain text)
5. Add notification preferences and unsubscribe

Pseudo-code:
```python
from enum import Enum
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
import smtplib
from email.mime.multipart import MIMEMultipart
from email.mime.text import MIMEText
from jinja2 import Template

class NotificationChannel(Enum):
    IN_APP = 'in_app'
    EMAIL = 'email'
    SMS = 'sms'  # Future

class AlertEngine:
    def __init__(self):
        self.cooldown_period = timedelta(hours=4)  # Prevent spam
        
    def should_trigger(self, alert: WatchlistAlert, new_data: Dict) -> bool:
        # Check cooldown
        if alert.last_triggered:
            if datetime.now() - alert.last_triggered < self.cooldown_period:
                return False
                
        # Check condition
        if alert.alert_type == 'price_change':
            return self._check_price_condition(alert, new_data)
        elif alert.alert_type == 'volume_spike':
            return self._check_volume_condition(alert, new_data)
        elif alert.alert_type == 'news_event':
            return bool(new_data.get('news', []))
            
    def trigger_alert(self, user_id: int, alert: WatchlistAlert, data: Dict):
        # Create notification record
        notification = Notification(
            user_id=user_id,
            alert_id=alert.id,
            title=self._generate_title(alert, data),
            body=self._generate_body(alert, data),
            data=data,
            is_read=False,
            created_at=datetime.now()
        )
        db.session.add(notification)
        
        # Update alert last triggered
        alert.last_triggered = datetime.now()
        
        # Queue delivery
        for channel in alert.notification_channels:
            if channel == NotificationChannel.IN_APP.value:
                # Real-time via WebSocket
                send_websocket_notification(user_id, notification)
            elif channel == NotificationChannel.EMAIL.value:
                queue_email_notification.delay(notification.id)
                
        db.session.commit()

class EmailNotificationService:
    def __init__(self):
        self.smtp_config = get_smtp_config()
        self.templates = {
            'alert': Template(open('templates/alert_email.html').read()),
            'digest': Template(open('templates/digest_email.html').read())
        }
        
    def send_alert_email(self, notification: Notification):
        user = notification.user
        alert = notification.alert
        
        # Render bilingual email
        html_content = self.templates['alert'].render(
            user_name=user.name,
            ticker=alert.watchlist_item.ticker,
            alert_type=alert.alert_type,
            data=notification.data,
            timestamp=notification.created_at,
            unsubscribe_url=f'/api/alerts/unsubscribe/{user.unsubscribe_token}'
        )
        
        msg = MIMEMultipart('alternative')
        msg['Subject'] = f'TAM Research Alert: {alert.watchlist_item.ticker}'
        msg['From'] = 'alerts@tamcapital.com'
        msg['To'] = user.email
        
        msg.attach(MIMEText(html_content, 'html'))
        
        # Send
        with smtplib.SMTP(self.smtp_config['host'], self.smtp_config['port']) as server:
            server.starttls()
            server.login(self.smtp_config['user'], self.smtp_config['pass'])
            server.send_message(msg)

@app.task
def send_daily_digest():
    """Send daily email digest of all alerts"""
    service = EmailNotificationService()
    
    # Get users with email notifications enabled
    users = db.query(User).filter(
        User.notification_preferences['email_digest'] == True
    ).all()
    
    for user in users:
        # Get yesterday's notifications
        notifications = db.query(Notification).filter(
            Notification.user_id == user.id,
            Notification.created_at >= datetime.now() - timedelta(days=1)
        ).all()
        
        if notifications:
            service.send_digest_email(user, notifications)
```

tasks.title: Implement End-to-End Testing Suite
tasks.description: Create comprehensive test suite covering the complete research workflow from ticker input through all output formats
tasks.details:
Build automated test suite:
1. Set up Pytest with fixtures for test data
2. Create integration tests for full workflow
3. Implement output validation tests
4. Add performance benchmarks
5. Create test documentation

Test implementation:
```python
# tests/test_e2e_workflow.py
import pytest
from fastapi.testclient import TestClient
import asyncio
from pathlib import Path
import PyPDF2
from docx import Document
from pptx import Presentation

class TestResearchWorkflow:
    @pytest.fixture
    def test_tickers(self):
        return ['2222.SR', '7010.SR', '2010.SR']  # ARAMCO, STC, SABIC
        
    @pytest.fixture
    def authenticated_client(self, client: TestClient):
        # Login and return client with auth headers
        response = client.post('/api/auth/login', json={
            'email': 'test@tamcapital.com',
            'password': 'testpass123'
        })
        token = response.json()['access_token']
        client.headers['Authorization'] = f'Bearer {token}'
        return client
        
    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_complete_research_generation(self, authenticated_client, test_tickers):
        for ticker in test_tickers:
            # 1. Start research chat
            chat_response = authenticated_client.post('/api/chat/sessions', json={
                'ticker': ticker,
                'initial_message': f'Generate comprehensive research report for {ticker}'
            })
            assert chat_response.status_code == 201
            session_id = chat_response.json()['id']
            
            # 2. Wait for analysis completion
            max_wait = 120  # seconds
            start_time = asyncio.get_event_loop().time()
            
            while asyncio.get_event_loop().time() - start_time < max_wait:
                status_response = authenticated_client.get(f'/api/chat/sessions/{session_id}/status')
                status = status_response.json()['status']
                
                if status == 'completed':
                    break
                elif status == 'failed':
                    pytest.fail(f'Research generation failed for {ticker}')
                    
                await asyncio.sleep(5)
            else:
                pytest.fail(f'Research generation timed out for {ticker}')
                
            # 3. Get generated report
            report_response = authenticated_client.get(f'/api/reports/session/{session_id}')
            assert report_response.status_code == 200
            report_id = report_response.json()['id']
            
            # 4. Test all output formats
            for format in ['docx', 'pdf', 'pptx']:
                export_response = authenticated_client.get(f'/api/reports/{report_id}/export/{format}')
                assert export_response.status_code == 200
                
                # Save file for validation
                output_path = Path(f'test_outputs/{ticker}_{report_id}.{format}')
                output_path.parent.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
                output_path.write_bytes(export_response.content)
                
                # Validate output
                self._validate_output(output_path, format, ticker)
                
    def _validate_output(self, file_path: Path, format: str, ticker: str):
        if format == 'docx':
            doc = Document(file_path)
            # Check TAM letterhead
            assert any('TAM Capital' in p.text for p in doc.paragraphs[:5])
            # Check ticker mentioned
            assert any(ticker in p.text for p in doc.paragraphs)
            # Check minimum length
            assert len(doc.paragraphs) > 50
            
        elif format == 'pdf':
            with open(file_path, 'rb') as f:
                pdf = PyPDF2.PdfReader(f)
                # Check page count
                assert len(pdf.pages) >= 10
                # Check text content
                text = ''.join(page.extract_text() for page in pdf.pages)
                assert 'TAM Capital' in text
                assert ticker in text
                
        elif format == 'pptx':
            prs = Presentation(file_path)
            # Check slide count
            assert len(prs.slides) >= 10
            # Check title slide
            title_slide = prs.slides[0]
            assert any('TAM' in shape.text for shape in title_slide.shapes if hasattr(shape, 'text'))
            # Check for charts
            chart_count = sum(1 for slide in prs.slides 
                            for shape in slide.shapes 
                            if shape.has_chart)
            assert chart_count >= 3
            
    @pytest.mark.benchmark
    def test_generation_performance(self, authenticated_client, benchmark):
        def generate_report():
            response = authenticated_client.post('/api/chat/sessions', json={
                'ticker': '2222.SR',
                'initial_message': 'Generate report'
            })
            return response.json()
            
        result = benchmark(generate_report)
        # Assert generation initiated within 2 seconds
        assert benchmark.stats['mean'] < 2.0
        
    def test_arabic_content_generation(self, authenticated_client):
        # Test bilingual output
        response = authenticated_client.post('/api/reports/generate', json={
            'ticker': '2222.SR',
            'language': 'ar'
        })
        assert response.status_code in [200, 201]
        
        # Download Arabic PDF
        report_id = response.json()['id']
        pdf_response = authenticated_client.get(f'/api/reports/{report_id}/export/pdf')
        
        # Validate Arabic content
        pdf_path = Path(f'test_outputs/arabic_test.pdf')
        pdf_path.write_bytes(pdf_response.content)
        
        with open(pdf_path, 'rb') as f:
            pdf = PyPDF2.PdfReader(f)
            text = pdf.pages[0].extract_text()
            # Check for Arabic characters
            assert any('\u0600' <= char <= '\u06FF' for char in text)

# tests/conftest.py
import pytest
from sqlalchemy import create_engine
from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker

@pytest.fixture(scope='session')
def test_db():
    engine = create_engine('postgresql://test:test@localhost/tams_test')
    # Run migrations
    alembic_cfg = Config('alembic.ini')
    command.upgrade(alembic_cfg, 'head')
    
    yield engine
    
    # Cleanup
    command.downgrade(alembic_cfg, 'base')
```

Uses collaborative filtering on user_activity data.

3. Test related tickers are from same/similar sectors
4. Test suggestions update when user behavior changes
5. Test cold start: new user with no history gets sector-based defaults
6. Test dashboard widget renders suggestions correctly

tasks.title: Build Predictive Signals System (Experimental)
tasks.description: Create experimental ML signals: earnings surprise probability, momentum score, and risk signal. Display as 'AI Signals' badges on ticker cards with confidence levels. Clearly labeled as experimental with CMA compliance disclaimers.
tasks.details: Create ml/predictive_signals.py. Earnings Surprise: analyze historical earnings vs estimates pattern from yfinance, combine with current sentiment score, output probability (0-100%). Momentum Score: composite of 14-day price momentum + volume trend (20-day) + sentiment trend, normalized 0-100. Risk Signal: combines VaR percentile + sentiment decline rate + volume anomaly score, outputs low/medium/high with score. Display as colored badges on ticker cards and research page. Every signal includes: confidence level, calculation timestamp, disclaimer text. CMA compliance: 'This is an AI-generated experimental signal and does not constitute investment advice.'
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Test each signal produces valid output range
2. Test signals update when new data available
3. Test badge display on ticker cards
4. Test disclaimer is always visible
5. Test with tickers that have limited history (edge case)
6. Backtest signals against historical data for basic validation

tasks.title: Build Research Notes System
tasks.description: Allow analysts to attach timestamped markdown notes to any ticker. Notes are searchable, taggable, and visible on report detail and ticker overview pages. Includes pin-to-top functionality for important notes.
tasks.details: Create data/research_notes.py with NotesDAO for CRUD operations. UI: notes panel on Research page sidebar and ticker detail view. Each note: markdown content, tags (multi-select), pinned status, timestamp, author. New note form: text area + tag selector + save button. Notes list: sorted by pinned first, then date. Search bar: full-text search across all notes for the user. Tags: predefined (Bullish, Bearish, Catalyst, Risk, Earnings, Technical) + custom. Display author avatar + name for team visibility.
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Test creating, editing, deleting notes
2. Test notes appear on correct ticker pages
3. Test search returns matching notes
4. Test tag filtering
5. Test pin/unpin functionality
6. Test notes persist across sessions via Supabase

tasks.title: Build Scheduled Report Generation
tasks.description: Allow users to configure recurring report generation: weekly watchlist refresh, monthly portfolio review, or custom ticker/schedule combinations. Uses APScheduler with Supabase-backed schedule storage. Generated reports are auto-stored and optionally emailed.
tasks.details: Create services/scheduler.py using APScheduler with Supabase job store. Schedule types: weekly watchlist refresh (regenerate all watched ticker reports), monthly portfolio review (comprehensive portfolio analysis), custom (any ticker, any cron schedule). UI: schedule manager page showing active schedules with next run time, toggle on/off, edit/delete. When triggered: run analysis silently, store report in Supabase, send email notification with PDF attachment (optional). Handle failures gracefully: retry once, then alert user.
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Test creating weekly schedule
2. Test schedule triggers at correct time
3. Test generated report is stored correctly
4. Test email notification sent on completion
5. Test schedule can be paused and resumed
6. Test failure handling and retry logic
7. Test multiple concurrent scheduled jobs

tasks.title: Build Audit Trail and Compliance Logging
tasks.description: Log every significant user action (report generation, exports, logins, admin changes, data modifications) to an append-only audit log in Supabase. Build admin-facing audit log viewer with filtering. Meets CMA regulatory record-keeping requirements.
tasks.details: Create data/audit_log.py with log_action(user_id, action, resource, details) function. Actions to log: login/logout, report generated, report exported (which format), watchlist modified, portfolio modified, alert rule created/modified, admin actions (user invite, role change, deactivation), system config changes. Audit table: append-only (no UPDATE/DELETE permissions via RLS). Admin viewer: filterable table with columns for timestamp, user, action, resource, details. Export audit log to CSV/Excel for compliance submissions.
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Test all action types are logged
2. Test log entries cannot be modified or deleted
3. Test admin viewer displays correct data
4. Test filtering by user, action, date range
5. Test export to CSV
6. Test high-volume logging doesn't impact performance

tasks.title: Implement Research Cancel/Stop Functionality
tasks.description: Add the ability to cancel an in-progress research analysis. When a user triggers analysis for the wrong ticker or wants to abort, they can click a 'Stop' button that cancels the Claude API call and returns to the chat input state without waiting for the full generation to complete.
tasks.details: Modify the analysis pipeline in app.py to support cancellation. Use threading: run analysis in a background thread, check a cancel flag periodically between section generations. Add a 'Stop Research' button (red, prominent) that appears during analysis progress. When clicked: set cancel flag, abort current API call (anthropic client supports cancel), clean up partial results, show 'Research cancelled' message. Partial results: offer to keep what was generated so far or discard entirely. Reset chat input state for new query.
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Test cancel button appears during analysis
2. Test clicking cancel stops generation within 5 seconds
3. Test partial results are offered to user
4. Test discarding partial results cleans up properly
5. Test new analysis can start immediately after cancel
6. Test cancel during different stages (section 1, section 5, etc.)

tasks.title: Add Interactive Pre-Analysis Questions
tasks.description: Before running any research, sector analysis, or comparison, ask the user clarifying questions to tailor the output: investment horizon, risk tolerance, specific focus areas, comparison metrics preference, language preference (Arabic/English/Both). Display as a glass-styled question card with smart defaults.
tasks.details: Create a pre-analysis questionnaire system. For single stock research: ask about investment horizon (short/medium/long-term), focus areas (fundamentals only, technicals only, both, specific sections), risk appetite, output language. For sector analysis: ask about which metrics to emphasize, peer group size, include ESG? For comparison: ask which metrics to compare, weighting preferences, visualization preference (table vs chart). UI: glass card with radio buttons / multi-select checkboxes, 'Quick Start' button that uses defaults, 'Customize' that shows full options. Store user's last preferences as defaults for next time. Inject selected preferences into the Claude prompt for customized output.
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Test question card appears before research
2. Test 'Quick Start' uses saved defaults
3. Test 'Customize' shows full options
4. Test selected preferences affect Claude output
5. Test preferences persist between sessions
6. Test for each analysis type (research, sector, comparison)
7. Test Arabic/English language selection works

tasks.title: Build Peer Benchmarking Heat Map
tasks.description: Create an interactive heat map visualization that compares a stock against its sector peers across 15+ financial ratios. Color-coded cells show relative ranking (green=top quartile, red=bottom quartile). Embedded in Research and Sectors pages.
tasks.details: Create data/peer_benchmark.py module. For a given ticker, identify sector peers from config.py TADAWUL_SECTORS mapping. Pull key metrics for all peers via yfinance: P/E, P/B, EV/EBITDA, ROE, ROA, debt/equity, dividend yield, revenue growth, profit margin, current ratio, beta, market cap, 52-week return, volume, free cash flow yield. Build Plotly heatmap: rows=tickers, columns=metrics, color scale=relative ranking within peer group. Highlight the target ticker's row. Add sort-by-metric capability. Export to Excel.
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Test peer identification for each Tadawul sector
2. Test metrics pulled for all peers
3. Test heatmap renders with correct colors
4. Test target ticker row is highlighted
5. Test sort functionality
6. Test with sectors of different sizes (2 peers vs 8 peers)
7. Test Excel export includes all data

tasks.title: Build Financial Statement Interactive Viewer
tasks.description: Create an interactive 5-year financial statement viewer (income statement, balance sheet, cash flow) with expandable line items, growth rate calculations, margin analysis, and trend sparklines. Displayed as glass-styled tables with Plotly mini-charts.
tasks.details: Create data/financial_viewer.py module. Pull 5-year annual financials from yfinance (income_stmt, balance_sheet, cashflow). Display as expandable HTML tables with: line item name, 5 years of values, YoY growth %, CAGR, sparkline trend. Three tabs: Income Statement, Balance Sheet, Cash Flow. Key metrics highlighted: revenue, net income, total assets, free cash flow. Margins calculated inline: gross margin, operating margin, net margin. All numbers formatted with locale-aware formatting (SAR thousands/millions). Glass card styling consistent with TAM Liquid Glass theme.
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Test data loads for Saudi tickers
2. Test all three statement types display correctly
3. Test growth rate calculations are accurate
4. Test sparklines render correctly
5. Test expandable sections toggle properly
6. Test with tickers missing some data (graceful handling)

tasks.title: Fix Landing Page for Light Theme
tasks.description: Fix all hardcoded color:white references in landing.py hero/headings so text is visible on light background
tasks.details: Change color:white to C_TEXT (#0F172A) on hero heading (line 105), 'Everything You Need' (line 138), fallback TAM CAPITAL (line 90). Fix footer border from rgba(255,255,255,0.05) to C_BORDER.
tasks.testStrategy: Load landing page on live site. All text must be readable on #F8FAFC background. No white-on-white text.

tasks.title: Diagnose and Fix API Rate Limit Error
tasks.description: Fix the 'Too Many Requests' error that occurs immediately on first query even after hours of inactivity
tasks.details: Add API key validation on startup. Better error classification: distinguish no key / invalid key / real rate limit / server error. Show model name in error for debugging. Verify API key loads correctly from Streamlit secrets.
tasks.testStrategy: Submit a simple research query. Should either work or show a specific, helpful error message.

tasks.title: Make Quick Suggestion Chips Clickable
tasks.description: Convert HTML-only suggestion chips on Research page to native st.button() that auto-submit as research queries
tasks.details: Replace HTML span chips with st.button() calls. On click, set st.session_state.quick_prompt to chip text and rerun. Style as pill buttons matching .quick-chip CSS.
tasks.testStrategy: Click each chip. Should immediately submit that text as a research query.

tasks.title: Fix Sector Analyze Buttons Styling
tasks.description: Fix washed-out sector 'Analyze' buttons to use TAM gradient and ensure they are functional
tasks.details: Ensure sector buttons use type='primary' for gradient styling. Verify each triggers correct analysis. Remove any non-functional buttons.
tasks.testStrategy: Click each sector Analyze button. Should trigger analysis or navigate. Button text must be readable.

tasks.title: Fix Compare Page Button Styling
tasks.description: Fix 'Run Comparison' and quick comparison buttons that appear washed out on light theme
tasks.details: Ensure Run Comparison uses primary type. Fix quick comparison buttons to be visible with readable text on light background.
tasks.testStrategy: All buttons on Compare page must have readable text and gradient/bordered styling.

tasks.title: Fix Chat Input Dark Background Strip
tasks.description: Remove dark navy background strip behind chat input that clashes with light theme
tasks.details: Check CSS and inline styles for dark background on chat input container. Should blend with light page background.
tasks.testStrategy: Chat input area should not have a visually jarring dark strip. Should blend with the page.

tasks.title: Global Button Audit — Remove Non-Clickable Buttons
tasks.description: Audit every button across all pages. Remove or fix any non-functional/decorative buttons.
tasks.details: Check all pages: landing, research, dashboard, sectors, compare, portfolio, watchlist, alerts. Every button must have a working click handler. Replace HTML fake buttons with native st.button(). All primary buttons get TAM gradient, secondary buttons get visible borders.
tasks.testStrategy: Click every button on every page. Each must either do something or not exist.

tasks.title: Sidebar Footer Contrast Fix
tasks.description: Verify sidebar footer text is readable on light background
tasks.details: Check 'TAM Capital | CMA Regulated' and 'Confidential - Internal Use Only' text colors against light sidebar.
tasks.testStrategy: Footer text must be visible on the #FAFBFC sidebar background.
